title: Wire local-client injected locations into unified provider
description: Why this exists: project-LICHEN-zte2 acceptance requires app/local-client injected locations to use the same firmware-wide location representation as hardware, network, and manual sources. The core HAL provider API exists, but no native local-client or compatibility app path submits location samples yet. What needs to be done: identify the LCI/Meshtastic/app location injection entry points, translate incoming location/fix metadata into lichen_hal_location_sample with source_class LOCAL_CLIENT, enforce freshness/accuracy/source-name policy, and add native_sim/qemu tests. Acceptance criteria: a no-hardware test injects a local-client/app location and observes it through lichen_hal_location_time_snapshot_get(); invalid partial fixes and stale client locations behave consistently with the core provider; two code-review passes are completed.
notes: Revised review outcomes for local-client bridge slice: Averroes found no P0/P1/P2 after fixes; Bohr raised P2 scope that CoAP/Meshtastic endpoint parsing remains separate from the new app_interface_submit_location() ingress; Faraday found no code P0/P1 and requested parent dependency coverage for Meshtastic follow-up. Created/kept follow-ups project-LICHEN-zte2.6 for native LCI CoAP location endpoint and project-LICHEN-zte2.7 for Meshtastic POSITION_APP decoding; parent project-LICHEN-zte2 now depends on both. Focused EC2 Twister command passed: west twister -T lichen/tests/app_interface -T lichen/tests/hal -p native_sim -p qemu_x86 --inline-logs --integration --outdir twister-out-local-client-location-v1 -x=ZEPHYR_EXTRA_MODULES=$PWD/lichen. Result: 5/6 configurations passed, 1 static skip, 83 test cases executed, 23 skipped, 0 failures, 0 errors, 0 warnings, 97.45 seconds.
